title: New draft bill on pay transparency
excerpt: Draft Bill UC127 implements EU Directive 2023/970. Employers will be required to establish transparent pay systems and provide employees with access to information on average pay.

title: Entry into force of the new regulations on the KSC
excerpt: The Act transposing the NIS2 Directive comes into force on 3 April 2026. A significantly wider range of entities must register with the KSC Register and implement an information security management system.

title: How do you elect employee representatives in a company without a trade union?
excerpt: The draft bill on pay transparency requires consultation with employee representatives. The bill does not specify the selection procedure — we explain how to carry out this process safely.

title: New obligations arising at the intersection of new technologies and food law
excerpt: Regulation 2024/1689, known as the AI Act, establishes an EU framework for the use of artificial intelligence. For the food industry, this means analysing the extent to which AI is used and, in specific cases, the obligation to disclose this on the packaging.

title: Judgment of the CJEU in Case C-744/24
excerpt: The Court of Justice of the European Union has confirmed that banks may not charge interest on the costs of a loan. The ruling paves the way for challenging loan agreements and seeking compensation for interest-free loans.
